source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from tracked rescue-robot chassis practice (NIST ASTM E2592 test arenas, RoboCupRescue Robot League): aluminium extrusion baseplate, four drive-motor mounts, upper chassis plate as sensor deck, flipper arms with hinge pin, sensor tower, bumper frame, and fasteners. Load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds inherit verbatim from the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the chassis of a Rescue-league robot for an urban-search-and-rescue robotics benchmark. The robot size class is Small (bounding box within 500 mm x 500 mm x 500 mm). Total robot mass must not exceed 20 kg. The chassis must traverse a standard Rescue arena ramp at 45 degrees incline and a stair-step obstacle with step height 150 mm and step depth 250 mm. The robot carries a 5 kg payload (sensor package) on its top deck. Under a 2 g vertical shock (landing off the top of a step) the chassis must not plastically yield, and the top deck must not deflect more than 3 mm. Under a lateral impact of 100 N at the payload CG the chassis must not yield. Flipper arms (if present) must carry the robot's full weight when elevating a wheel; worst-case flipper root stress is evaluated with the full 20 kg weight applied 200 mm out from the flipper hinge. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for self-weight-on-flipper, 2 g vertical shock, and lateral impact cases. Kinematic reachability of the 45-deg ramp and 150 mm step is computed from CAD (non-FEA). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the chassis + flipper arm, a mass budget, and a report listing total robot mass in kg, max chassis stress per case in MPa, top deck deflection at 2 g in mm, and flipper root stress in MPa.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This rescue-robot chassis is delivered as a bolted-extrusion multi-part assembly typical of tracked RoboCupRescue platforms. The speculative decomposition is: (A) aluminium baseplate, (B) four drive-motor mount brackets for left/right track modules (front+rear), (C) upper chassis plate / sensor top deck, (D) battery tray with hold-downs, (E) sensor tower mounting a LiDAR/PTZ camera head, (F) bumper frame ringing the front and sides, (G) flipper arm pair hinged at baseplate corners, and (H) M4/M5 fastener set. Inter-family interfaces: motor-mounts bolted to baseplate, baseplate bolted to side rails of upper chassis plate, sensor tower bolted to upper plate, battery tray bolted to baseplate, flipper arm pinned at hinge boss bolted to baseplate corner, bumper frame bolted around baseplate perimeter. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part DCR at motor-mount bolts, flipper-hinge pin, and sensor-tower base ≤ 1.0 under LC1/LC2/LC3. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis of a Rescue-league robot as a multi-part bolted-extrusion assembly (baseplate, four drive-motor mounts, upper sensor deck, battery tray, sensor tower, bumper frame, flipper arm pair, M4/M5 fasteners).

Envelope 500 mm x 500 mm x 500 mm; total robot mass <= 20 kg; carries a 5 kg payload on the top deck. Chassis must traverse a 45 degrees ramp and a step of 150 mm height x 250 mm depth.

Load cases: LC1 2 g vertical shock (no yield; top deck deflection <= 3 mm); LC2 100 N lateral at payload CG (no yield); LC3 flipper root with 20 kg weight applied 200 mm out from the hinge (196 N). FEM verification runs on the assembled model only; connection DCR <= 1.0 at motor-mount bolts, flipper-hinge pin, and sensor-tower base.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Ramp traversal and step-climbing are geometric, not FEA.

prompt.title: Rescue-league robot chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 20 kg, 500 mm-envelope tracked rescue robot chassis as an assembly of baseplate, four drive-motor mounts, upper sensor deck, battery tray, sensor tower, bumper frame, flipper arms, and fasteners; climbs 45 deg ramps and 150 mm steps, survives 2 g vertical shock and 100 N lateral impact. FEM at assembled level; connection DCR ≤ 1.0.

parts.name: baseplate
parts.role: primary chassis plate carrying motor mounts, battery tray, and flipper hinges

parts.name: drive-motor mount bracket
parts.role: secures drive motor / gearbox of each track module; transfers drive torque and wheel reaction into baseplate

parts.name: upper chassis plate / sensor top deck
parts.role: top deck carrying 5 kg payload, constrained to ≤ 3 mm deflection under 2 g vertical shock
parts.material_hint: CFRP plate or Al 6061-T6 with milled pockets

parts.name: battery tray
parts.role: cradles LiPo battery pack, hold-downs react 2 g shock on battery mass
parts.material_hint: Al 6063-T5 bent sheet with Velcro + strap or bolted lid

parts.name: sensor tower
parts.role: pylon mounting LiDAR/PTZ camera head above chassis; cantilevered under 100 N lateral impact LC2
parts.material_hint: Al 20x20 or 30x30 extrusion (e.g. Misumi HFS) bolted to upper plate

parts.name: bumper frame
parts.role: protective perimeter frame absorbs impacts at front and sides; not primary load path
parts.material_hint: Al 6063-T5 bent tube, bolted around baseplate perimeter

parts.name: flipper arm pair
parts.role: articulated arms extending forward of drive tracks; carry full 20 kg weight cantilevered 200 mm from hinge in LC3
parts.material_hint: Al 7075-T6 machined plate, steel hinge pin hardened to 55 HRC

parts.name: fastener set (M4/M5)
parts.role: joins all structural panels and brackets
parts.material_hint: Stainless A2-70 or zinc-plated steel with thread-locker

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: origin at baseplate center-bottom, X forward drive direction, Z up

pass_fail_criteria.description: DCR at motor-mount bolts, flipper-hinge pin, sensor-tower base, and baseplate-to-upper-plate side-rail bolts ≤ 1.0 under LC1/LC2/LC3 (worst-case envelope).
Flipper-hinge pin shear stress ≤ 0.6 x steel yield under LC3 evaluated on assembled model (pin-joint contact).

interfaces.load_path: Each drive-motor mount bracket bolts to baseplate motor pad; transfers wheel reaction and drive torque into baseplate
Baseplate connects to upper chassis plate via side rails / vertical posts bolted at corners
Battery tray bolted to baseplate with hold-down screws reacting 2 g battery inertia
Sensor tower base bolted to upper chassis plate with 4x M5; reacts lateral impact LC2
Bumper frame bolted around baseplate perimeter through mounting lugs
Flipper arm hinges on steel pin in baseplate corner boss; carries LC3 cantilever moment
Motor mount bolted to drive-module housing via fastener set H

fem_check_policy.rationale: Top-deck deflection (R4) depends on baseplate-to-upper-plate coupling and sensor-tower stiffness; flipper root stress (R3 applied to LC3) requires the pin-joint interface and baseplate corner boss together. Per-part FEA misses these coupled load paths.

notes.why_multi_part: Tracked rescue robots are inherently modular: drive modules, sensor head, battery, and flipper arms are field-swappable. NIST ASTM E2592 maintainability requirements implicitly assume a bolted multi-part architecture.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts and dimensions reflect typical RoboCupRescue Small-class tracked robots (e.g. Hibikino-Musashi, Pelican Rescue, Shinobi); specific drivetrain and sensor payload may alter part count.
